title: A friend needs support
desc: Comfort someone having a hard time
setting: Your friend Lukas seems down and finally opens up to you.

steps.speaker: Lukas
steps.es: Sorry, ich bi grad nöd so guet druf. Schwiirigi Wuche.
steps.en: Sorry, I'm not in a great mood. Tough week.

options.es: Hei, erzäll, was isch los. Ich lose zue.
options.en: Hey, tell me what's going on. I'm listening.
options.feedback: Warm and open — offering to listen is the best first move.
Direct and caring. Invites him to share.
Means well but brushes past his feelings. Let him talk first.

steps.speaker: Lukas
steps.es: Uf de Arbet lauft's grad gar nöd. Ich bi voll im Stress.
steps.en: Work is going badly right now. I'm totally stressed.

options.es: Das tönt würkli streng. Mues hart sii grad.
options.en: That sounds really tough. Must be hard right now.
options.feedback: Validating his feeling before fixing — exactly what a friend does.

options.en: Do you want to talk about it?
options.feedback: Gentle and giving him space. Kind.
This dismisses his pain — never helpful. Stay with how he feels.

steps.speaker: Lukas
steps.es: Merci, dass d'eifach zuelosisch. Das tuet scho guet.
steps.en: Thanks for just listening. That already helps.

options.en: Always. That's what friends are for.
options.feedback: Simple and true — the perfect thing to say.
A bit dismissive of his thanks. Let the warmth land.

steps.speaker: Lukas
steps.es: Sölle mir am Wucheänd öppis mache, zum abschalte?
steps.en: Should we do something this weekend, to switch off?

options.es: Unbedingt! Mir gönd go wandere, das tuet dir guet.
options.en: Definitely! Let's go hiking, that'll do you good.
options.feedback: Caring and concrete — turning support into a plan. Excellent.

options.es: Ja, gärn! Ich plane öppis Schöns.
options.en: Yes, gladly! I'll plan something nice.
options.feedback: Generous and warm. A real friend's reply.
He reached out — a clearer yes would mean more to him right now.
